title: Fix activeStream null crash in setupAnalyserNodes
description:
# Fix activeStream null crash in setupAnalyserNodes

## 📝 User Story / Description
When Calibration Mode activates, `state.activeStream` is intentionally nullified to shut down the physical microphone loopback. However, `setupAnalyserNodes` attempts to query `state.activeStream.getAudioTracks()` without null-checking, causing a `TypeError`.

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `setupAnalyserNodes` safely handles null `state.activeStream` via optional chaining or explicit null checks.

## 🛠️ Technical Notes
- Update `src/audio.ts` at line 214 to `state.activeStream?.getAudioTracks()[0]?.getSettings();`

## 🛡️ Rigor & Verification Boundary
- [ ] Clicking the Calibration Mode toggle activates test signals without crashing.

- **Quality Debt Exemptions:**
  - `complexity_limit: false`
  - `doc_drift: false`
  - `dry_candidate: false`

### 📝 Git Commit Details
```
Fix activeStream null crash in setupAnalyserNodes

Reasoning:
When Calibration Mode activates, the application intentionally nullifies the physical microphone stream loopback to inject the synthetic oscillator. This caused `setupAnalyserNodes` to crash with a TypeError when it subsequently attempted to query the detached stream for channel properties.

Changes:
- Added optional chaining to `state.activeStream?.getAudioTracks()` in `src/audio.ts` to safely bypass channel evaluation when in diagnostic mode.
```

### 📖 Walkthrough / Specs Checklist
# Fix activeStream null crash in setupAnalyserNodes

## Acceptance Criteria Coverage
- Added optional chaining (`?.`) to `state.activeStream` in `setupAnalyserNodes` within `src/audio.ts`. This gracefully handles situations where the active stream has been intentionally detached, such as when Calibration Signal mode starts.

## Verification
- Code successfully builds via `tsc`.
- Activating the Calibration Signal toggle correctly spawns the internal oscillator without triggering a `TypeError` in the console.
